segments.text: [Music]
jumla multiple xss vulnerabilities jumla
is a free and open source content
management system used for building
websites and online applications sonar's
vulnerability research team recently
discovered an issue that led to multiple
xss vulnerabilities in the popular CMS
here we set up jumla and Center cloud
and start
analyzing
and done we immediately see the xss
issuing the
report with connected mode with Center
lint we see the issue in the IDE and can
navigate deep into the actual
vulnerability
itself Jess clean tags method sanitizes
potentially malicious input by
determining the position of any opening
angle bracket and then removing all data
following until and including the
corresponding closing bracket the
removal of potentially malicious data is
done via two different methods the first
method determines the index of an
opening angle bracket this index is then
passed to the second method which
extracts the corresponding part of the
input string both of these methods are
just Trappers around the respective PHP
and B string functions when determining
if the sanitization is safe we notice
that both PHP functions handle invalid
utf8 sequences differently when mbst
posos encounters a utf8 leading bite it
tries to parse the following
continuation bites until the full bite
sequence is read if an invalid bite is
encountered all consumed bites are
considered one character and parsing is
started over again MB suub Sr on the
other hand skips over continuation bites
when encountering a leading bite
attackers can leverage this difference
to offset the index Beyond an opening
angle bracket and fully bypass juness
standardization here is a demonstration
from the perspective of both the
attacker and the victim which shows the
full impact of the
vulnerability jumer released a security
announcement and published version 503
which mitigates the vulnerability you
can read all the full technical details
on Sonar source.com
blog

fullText: [Music] jumla multiple xss vulnerabilities jumla is a free and open source content management system used for building websites and online applications sonar's vulnerability research team recently discovered an issue that led to multiple xss vulnerabilities in the popular CMS here we set up jumla and Center cloud and start analyzing and done we immediately see the xss issuing the report with connected mode with Center lint we see the issue in the IDE and can navigate deep into the actual vulnerability itself Jess clean tags method sanitizes potentially malicious input by determining the position of any opening angle bracket and then removing all data following until and including the corresponding closing bracket the removal of potentially malicious data is done via two different methods the first method determines the index of an opening angle bracket this index is then passed to the second method which extracts the corresponding part of the input string both of these methods are just Trappers around the respective PHP and B string functions when determining if the sanitization is safe we notice that both PHP functions handle invalid utf8 sequences differently when mbst posos encounters a utf8 leading bite it tries to parse the following continuation bites until the full bite sequence is read if an invalid bite is encountered all consumed bites are considered one character and parsing is started over again MB suub Sr on the other hand skips over continuation bites when encountering a leading bite attackers can leverage this difference to offset the index Beyond an opening angle bracket and fully bypass juness standardization here is a demonstration from the perspective of both the attacker and the victim which shows the full impact of the vulnerability jumer released a security announcement and published version 503 which mitigates the vulnerability you can read all the full technical details on Sonar source.com blog
